page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is a high-capacity server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 176 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 88 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores running at 2.7 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 704 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is a defining characteristic of this configuration, which bundles 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across 32 local SSD drives. No GPU accelerators are included in this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance and memory throughput. The instance achieves top-decile results in multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark CPU benchmarks, and memory bandwidth tests, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 445.45 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 194.75 GB/sec. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier across stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Database operations and web serving throughput are exceptionally strong, with Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both placing in the top 10%, the latter achieving 164.22 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-176-lssd offers high resource density, combining massive local storage with a high-core count on a single instance. This reduces the need for external storage attachments, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training. The instance is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput web serving, and intensive multi-threaded computing tasks such as software compilation and image processing. It also supports moderate LLM inference workloads, demonstrating strong prompt processing speeds but average text generation throughput.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is a high-capacity server featuring 176 dedicated vCPUs, 704 GB of RAM, and 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives. Powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside exceptional database and Redis transaction speeds. While single-core performance remains in the average tier, its massive local storage and high core density provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd features 176 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd offers 176 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 12.5 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-core-density server combining massive local NVMe SSD storage with top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for data-intensive enterprise workloads.

12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Excellent database operations and Redis transaction throughput
